segments.title: Japanese naval history beyond World War II
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke argues that Japan's naval past offers far more than its familiar World War II story, especially in earlier amphibious warfare and ship design.
segments.body: Reflecting on the Japan trip, Dr. Clarke says that searches for Japanese naval history too often narrow the subject to World War II. He points instead to a broader and more varied historical record worth investigating on its own terms. One theme that particularly interests him is the character of earlier Japanese ship designs. He describes them as closely shaped by amphibious warfare, with a functional elegance suited to moving and fighting across the littoral. The point is a useful prompt to look beyond the Pacific War when approaching Japanese naval history.

evidence.note: He says Japanese naval history is commonly reduced to World War II and highlights earlier designs shaped by amphibious warfare.
